definition: Fiscal deficits partially buffer the real economy from QT through the Kalecki-Levy profit equation. With 6-7% GDP government deficits, aggregate corporate profits are mechanically supported, explaining why QT has not yet produced a recession. The buffer operates on aggregates, not individual leveraged entities — a company facing margin calls is not saved by high aggregate profits.
evidence: Kalecki-Levy profit equation is an accounting identity, not a debatable analytical claim
6-7% GDP government deficits support aggregate corporate profits mechanically
Explains why QT has not produced a recession despite 45+ months
Buffer does not prevent financial stress from propagating through leveraged channels
Immunization framing overstates — the identity governs aggregates, not distribution
open_questions: Does the fiscal buffer prevent or merely delay financial stress from QT?
Is fiscal support robust enough to prevent a balance-sheet recession under acute financial stress?
Does the buffer merely extend the runway before a Minsky moment at higher leverage?
What happens when fiscal deficits narrow — does the buffer disappear?
